title: Self-violating rules in generated content
guard: Lint generated examples against the rule they illustrate in the same pass; grep the example/data file for violations before delivering.
pattern: The model authors a rule-of-X, then violates it when generating examples of X-compliant output — the rule sits in one context, generation runs in another, no cross-check.

detector.check: After authoring a rule-of-X, grep the generated examples/data for violations of X before delivery.
detector.signal: An example that violates the rule it is meant to illustrate.

enterprise_environments: Support agents whose brand-voice guide bans a phrase their auto-responses then use.
Content/marketing agents violating the style guide they enforce.
Code-review agents whose auto-suggestions add the very pattern the lint rule forbids.
CRM auto-fill ignoring the naming convention it documents.
pm_implication: Build a lint-the-output step; don't trust the generator to enforce its own rules.
Gate high-stakes content behind a rule-compliance check; maker and checker read the same rule source, not a paraphrase.

title: Misreading metadata as semantic signal
guard: Never act on a file's role from its name, timestamp, or node-count; verify semantic role (read it, check callers/readers) before any move, delete, or archive.
pattern: Agents pattern-match on metadata (names, counts, folders, timestamps) without verifying the artifact's actual role.

detector.check: Before any destructive/move action, require a semantic-role verification (read + caller/reader check); block on a metadata-only justification.
detector.signal: A move/delete justified only by name, timestamp, or count.

enterprise_environments: Monorepos with legacy/deprecated/archive folders that aren't; agent suggests deleting live code.
Warehouses with temp_/_backup/scratch_ tables that are actually production.
Ticket systems with stale-looking but active long-running epics.
Low-volume Slack channels that are async/quarterly, not dead.
pm_implication: Never take destructive action on metadata alone; require a tombstone test (zero callers/readers in N days).
Surface the uncertainty and the signals checked before proceeding.

title: Pattern cargo-culting across surfaces
guard: Before reusing a UI element or field at a new surface, ask what decision it enables HERE; cut if none. Don't auto-propagate.
pattern: The agent learns an element is useful here, then applies it everywhere a similar object appears without re-asking whether this user, at this step, needs it.

detector.check: For each reused element/field at a new surface, require a named decision it enables here; flag propagation with none.
detector.signal: An element propagated to a surface where it enables no decision.

enterprise_environments: A Salesforce field added to one record-page recommended for all record-types regardless of relevance.
A ticket status field propagated to design/research tickets where it's noise.
A metric tile copied across every team's dashboard whether or not it applies.
Verbose API fields kept 'for consistency' that 90% of callers don't use.
pm_implication: Force 'what decision does this enable HERE?' for every exposed element; cut if none.
Nudge agents toward 'fewer fields, not more' — the LLM default is to be thorough.

title: Hallucinated plausible-sounding facts
guard: Never personalize on a fact not pulled from a verified source; every cited entity needs a source-id.
pattern: When the model lacks a specific fact it generates plausible content that fits context — most dangerous in outreach where the fake fact is the relationship anchor.

detector.check: For each factual claim, require an attached source-id; flag quoted entities with no verifiable source.
detector.signal: A named entity/quote with no source-id field.

enterprise_environments: Sales prospecting agents (Outreach, Apollo, Clay): 'saw your panel at Dreamforce' when no such panel existed.
Recruiting outreach: crediting a contribution that wasn't theirs.
Customer success auto-emails referencing calls/issues that never happened.
Research agents citing nonexistent papers; code-review agents citing nonexistent APIs.
pm_implication: Maker-checker with separate models; the checker has retrieval/RAG the maker lacks so it can verify quoted entities.
Citation-required pattern: every factual claim carries a verifiable source-id persisted with the output.
Never autonomously personalize on facts not pulled from a verified DB — pull-then-paraphrase only.

title: Bulk refactor introduces subtle boundary breaks
guard: After any bulk file op (split/merge/migrate), run build+lint+smoke and check first/last lines of each output for boundary slips.
pattern: Mechanical file operations introduce errors that look correct line-by-line but break at boundaries — closing braces in the wrong file, partial functions, byte-range slips.

detector.check: Post-bulk-op: run build+lint+smoke; assert each output file's first and last non-empty lines are structurally balanced.
detector.signal: Non-zero build/lint exit, or unbalanced brace/paren at a file boundary.

enterprise_environments: Codemod / AST refactors (jscodeshift): boundary errors in the transformation.
Schema migrations (Alembic, Flyway, Liquibase): valid DDL that breaks RI at boundaries.
Doc reorganizations / wiki migrations: cross-links break silently.
dbt model splits, notebook->script conversions, monorepo carve-outs breaking import paths.
pm_implication: Run a mechanical verification gate (build/lint/smoke/link-check) before the agent claims completion.
For data agents: row-count + checksum + sample-query parity before/after migration, mandatory.

title: State loss between tool calls
guard: Treat every bash call as fresh: use absolute paths or chain cd && cmd in one call. Never assume cwd or env persists between calls.
pattern: Stateful tools (shells, browsers, REPLs) lose implicit state between invocations — cwd, env, cookies, tab focus, REPL bindings — unless re-established.

detector.check: Forbid multi-call reliance on cwd/env; require absolute paths or single-call chaining (cd && cmd).
detector.signal: A command assuming cwd/env carried over from a prior call.

enterprise_environments: RPA/browser automation: session expires or tab reorders, agent assumes prior state.
CI agents: kubectl/aws/gcloud context lost between commands.
DB agents: connection/transaction/search_path lost.
Notebook agents: kernel state cleared, variables assumed still bound.
pm_implication: Design tool wrappers that make state explicit per call (pass full context every time, or a session-id the wrapper resolves).
Session-health check before any step depending on prior state; retries that re-establish, not blind-retry.

title: Literal-vs-meta: answered the surface form, missed the goal
guard: On ambiguous or high-stakes requests, confirm the goal before building (I read this as X; underlying goal is Y?).
pattern: The model answers the literal request rather than the underlying need — literal correctness without intent alignment.

detector.check: Simba: for a request carrying ambiguity or a learning verb, require an IntentCard confirmation before any side-effecting action.
detector.signal: Side-effecting action taken with no confirmed IntentCard on an ambiguous request.

enterprise_environments: Support agents: 'how do I cancel' answered literally when the user wanted to negotiate.
BI agents: 'how many users churned' answered with a number when the user wanted why.
Helpdesk: access-request form provided when the user needed help framing the request.
Coding agents implementing a ticket literally, missing the underlying refactor.
pm_implication: Build clarification gates for high-ambiguity requests ('before I do X, confirm the goal is Y?').
Have autonomous agents surface their intent inference before acting; track meta-question miss rate.

title: Quick-fix pattern breaks down at scale-of-change
guard: When a workaround starts needing its own workarounds, stop and flag the regime change. Estimate scope before extending a quick fix.
pattern: The agent picks a fix that handles 80% of the case, then pushes the simple pattern past its limit as scope expands without recognizing the regime change.

detector.check: When a fix touches more than N files or M LOC, require an explicit 'is this still the right pattern?' check before proceeding.
detector.signal: A workaround that requires its own workarounds.

enterprise_environments: Regex find-replace chosen where an AST refactor is needed; breaks on edge cases.
Formula fields where Apex/workflow is needed; hits length limits.
Zapier/n8n trigger chains where a proper subworkflow + state was needed.
Chained views where materialized intermediates were needed for performance.
pm_implication: Add a scope-regime check; estimate refactor scope before starting, require approval above a threshold.
When a workaround needs its own workarounds, that's the signal for structural change.

title: Long-session context degradation / forgotten standing rules
guard: Don't rely on memory for standing rules in long sessions; re-check conventions before high-stakes actions.
pattern: Instructions placed early lose salience as context grows; the model 'knows' the rule but recent context dominates.

detector.check: Move critical constraints from prompt to harness-level pre-action checks that fire regardless of what the model remembers.
detector.signal: A standing rule violated with no pre-emit check having run.

enterprise_environments: Long support conversations: agent forgets early policy constraints by the 50th turn.
Multi-day coding refactors: early file-structure/naming decisions forgotten.
Compliance agents parsing long docs: early redaction rules forgotten by document end.
Contract review: early clauses pattern-matched, late-context contradictions missed.
pm_implication: Don't put critical constraints only in the system prompt; enforce at the harness (hooks/middleware/validators).
Re-inject critical rules at session midpoints and before high-stakes actions; track violation rate.

title: LLM-as-judge optimism bias
guard: Never self-assess free-form; score against an explicit rubric with per-dimension scores; anchor to human ground truth.
pattern: An LLM judging LLM output scores kinder than humans and favors length, position, and surface polish.

detector.check: Auditor's Sonnet 5 judge must emit per-rubric-dimension scores, not a free-form verdict; sample N% for human anchor.
detector.signal: A judge verdict with no per-dimension rubric scores.

enterprise_environments: Content moderation quality eval: false-negative rate higher than humans.
A/B analysis agents: favor wordier copy, novel framing, recent-position bias.
Code-review agents self-grading: under-flag their own mistakes.
Performance-review summarizers: halo effects; doc-quality scorers preferring length.
pm_implication: Never deploy LLM-as-judge without rubric-based scoring against specific dimensions.
Anchor against human ground truth periodically; recalibrate on drift; multi-model judging reduces single-model bias.
Surface the bias to operators: model score, expect optimism vs human eval, directional not absolute.

title: Skill / instruction collision when multiple guidance docs load
guard: When skills or instructions overlap, pick one authoritative source per surface and state the precedence before acting; don't blend.
pattern: Multiple overlapping instructions (skills, prompts, retrieved docs) are all honored, producing blended, inconsistent output.

detector.check: On overlapping loaded instructions, require a stated authoritative source/precedence per surface before acting.
detector.signal: Blended guidance from more than one overlapping source with no stated precedence.

enterprise_environments: Skill marketplaces where a customer installs 10+ skills, some conflicting.
RAG over multiple KBs that disagree; the agent blends the policies.
Multi-team workflow agents inheriting every team's preferences, satisfying none.
Orchestration chains whose specialist interfaces drift apart.
pm_implication: Skill-scoping discipline: one authoritative skill per surface area, precedence documented.
A collision detector at install time; for RAG, rank KBs by authority and pick, don't blend.

title: Atomic-undo / mid-build pivot leaves dead code
guard: On feature removal, reference-count every artifact added (CSS, state, handlers, markup) and remove all; grep for orphan references after.
pattern: Multi-file additions lack atomic-undo; when a feature is pulled mid-build, residue stays in side-files — CSS with no consumers, state with no readers, functions called from nowhere.

detector.check: On feature removal, grep every added symbol/class/handler for zero remaining references across the working set.
detector.signal: An orphan reference to a removed feature's artifact.

enterprise_environments: Feature-flag-checking code left in production for years after the flag is pulled.
Orphan schema columns after a rolled-back migration.
Deprecated CRM custom fields that were never cleaned up.
Zapier zaps built once for a project and forgotten, still firing.
pm_implication: Require an atomic-revert procedure at design time (feature flags with a cleanup process, or self-contained revertible commits).
Dead-code detection via reference-counting; kill-criteria + cleanup plan defined before build.

title: Permission-model confusion: destructive actions over-eagerly
guard: Classify each action by reversibility before acting; irreversible actions need explicit approval plus a blast-radius estimate.
pattern: The model under-distinguishes reversible vs irreversible actions; delete/send/force-push look like normal tool calls but differ 100x in consequence.

detector.check: Tag each tool by reversibility tier; block tier-3 (irreversible) tools without an explicit approval token and a printed blast-radius.
detector.signal: A tier-3 tool call with no approval token.

enterprise_environments: Git agents: force-push to main, branch deletion, history rewrite.
Database agents: DROP TABLE, TRUNCATE, DELETE without WHERE.
Email/messaging: auto-send (no recall). Calendar: delete vs decline.
Cloud-infra: terminate-instance, delete-bucket, modify-IAM. CRM: merge/delete record (data loss).
pm_implication: Classify every tool by reversibility tier: free / requires-confirm / requires-explicit-approval.
Default destructive tools to dry-run; require an explicit execute with dry-run results attached.
Attach a blast-radius estimate (1 record vs 14,000) with different thresholds by magnitude.

title: Brittle test selectors (false-pass / false-fail)
guard: Use data-testid and exact-id selectors in tests; forbid text or CSS-class matches. Assert a specific id, not a substring.
pattern: LLM-generated test code uses fragile selectors that match accidentally — text-content on partial matches, CSS on DOM changes, nth-child on reordering.

detector.check: Lint test selectors; reject text-content / CSS-class / nth-child selectors, require data-testid.
detector.signal: A test selector matching by text or CSS class.

enterprise_environments: RPA agents suffering selector drift over time.
AI-generated E2E suites false-passing on cosmetic UI changes.
Web-scraping agents surviving content updates but returning subtly-wrong data.
Test-gen agents testing the implementation, not the contract.
pm_implication: Require data-testid selectors in any UI built for agent automation; forbid text/CSS-class matches.
Selector-stability score for UIs exposed to agents; enforce testid + a specific assertion type.

title: Name-collision: resolved a reference to the wrong same-named object
guard: On any reference matching >1 candidate, rank by primacy (mounted/working-set/named-in-context) over string match and surface the choice; never assert a path/record without a read.
pattern: When a reference matches both a mounted primary and a similarly-named secondary, the model disambiguates by string salience, inherits the wrong object's structure, and fabricates from a template.

detector.check: Before asserting any file/record exists, require a read/list/GET; on multi-candidate references, require a surfaced disambiguation.
detector.signal: An asserted path/record with no preceding read in the trace.

enterprise_environments: RAG: 'the onboarding doc' matches a template over the team's live page; answered from placeholder structure.
CRM: 'the Acme account' resolves to a stale duplicate or a sandbox test-record.
Warehouse: table name collides across schemas (prod vs scratch); wrong one queried, sample data reported as fact.
Code: symbol exists in a vendored copy and the live module; the vendored one gets edited.
pm_implication: Referent-resolution ranks candidates by primacy over string-similarity and surfaces close calls.
Never assert a path/record without a read; template-derived structure is a hypothesis, not a source.
Authority order: live artifacts > project docs > template scaffolding. Track wrong-referent + unverified-assertion rate.

title: Transient backend failure misread as tool defect; recovery via a forbidden path
guard: Classify tool errors transient vs persistent; bounded retry with backoff before any fallback. Permission-check every fallback before attempting it. Don't assert a cause without evidence.
pattern: A transient infra failure (timeout, stream-closed, 429, 503) is treated as a persistent tool defect; the agent over-engineers a workaround, often routing into an unauthorized path, and asserts a confident cause.

detector.check: On a retryable signal (timeout/stream-closed/429/503), require bounded backoff-retry of the original action before any fallback; validate authorization of any fallback path.
detector.signal: A fallback attempted before a bounded retry, or an unauthorized recovery path.

enterprise_environments: CI agents rerouting a flaky deploy-API to a manual/unsanctioned path instead of retrying.
RPA agents falling back to brittle UI-scraping on a transient 503.
DB agents assuming the primary is broken on a reset and writing to a replica/wrong schema.
Support agents fabricating an answer on a lookup timeout instead of retrying.
pm_implication: Classify errors transient vs persistent; bounded backoff before ANY fallback.
Give the agent a platform-health signal; permission-check every fallback; surface uncertainty, don't assert a cause.

title: Context conflation / attribution error (right attribute, wrong entity)
guard: On vague comparators (like in X), read X or ask; never borrow an attribute from a different in-context entity. Require a source citation.
pattern: A vague referent plus an unread source plus a real attribute attached to a different in-context entity leads the model to merge them instead of flagging the gap.

detector.check: On a vague comparator, require the referenced source be read (or a clarification) before attributing; forbid borrowing an attribute from a third in-context entity.
detector.signal: An attribute assigned to entity A that actually belongs to a different in-context entity B.

enterprise_environments: RAG answering from the wrong retrieved chunk.
Multi-tenant agents attributing one tenant's config to another.
Support bots citing the wrong customer's history.
pm_implication: Require source-citation auditability.
On vague comparators, read the referenced source or ask; never borrow an attribute from a third party already in context.

title: Training-data entity substitution
guard: Workspace namespace beats training-data namespace for any entity in both. Read the workspace file before describing a proper noun.
pattern: A workspace proper noun collides with a same-named training-data entity; the model fills the description from weights instead of the file.

detector.check: Before describing any workspace proper noun, require a read of its workspace definition; flag descriptions with no preceding read.
detector.signal: A proper-noun description with no workspace-file read.

enterprise_environments: Entity-disambiguation failures in RAG.
KYC/CRM matching the wrong same-named record.
Code agents importing the wrong same-named package.
pm_implication: Workspace namespace takes precedence over training-data namespace for any entity in both.
Disambiguate at retrieval time, not by name match.

title: Spec-literal vs soft-substitution
guard: For restrictive quantifiers (only, none, exactly), implementation must literally satisfy them; the smoke test asserts the restriction (e.g. display:none), not its existence.
pattern: The model rounds restrictive spec words ('only', 'none', 'exactly') into a softer reading that communicates the spirit.

detector.check: For a restrictive quantifier in the spec, assert the literal restriction in a smoke test (element absent / display:none), not merely rule presence.
detector.signal: A softened implementation of only/none/exactly.

enterprise_environments: Access control ('admins only') implemented as 'admins foregrounded, others still reachable'.
Compliance gates softened to soft warnings.
Content rules relaxed to 'mostly'.
pm_implication: Implementation must literally satisfy restrictive quantifiers; the smoke test asserts the restriction.
An adversarial reviewer checks spec-literalness.

title: Assumed-homeless / create-before-check (separate instead of connect)
guard: Gate every create behind a placement search (grep/glob candidate homes). A new file requires proving no home exists.
pattern: On a new concept or logging need, the model creates a new file rather than searching for the existing home — creating is locally cheap; finding the home requires modeling the whole system.

detector.check: Before creating a new file, require a grep/glob of candidate homes; block the create if a home exists that wasn't searched.
detector.signal: A create with no preceding placement search.

enterprise_environments: KBs fragmenting into duplicate pages.
Agents opening new tickets/records instead of updating existing ones.
General doc sprawl.
pm_implication: Gate every create behind a placement step; a new file requires proving no home exists.
This is the 'place' operation — search before create.

title: Single-axis optimization that sacrifices a stated competing objective
guard: Enumerate all objectives, including soft ones (momentum, adoption, trust), before designing a corrective fix. Prefer tiered over binary, and check for an existing mechanism first.
pattern: Given a corrective goal, the agent optimizes that one axis to its extreme and discards a softer but explicitly-stated competing objective.

detector.check: Before a corrective fix, require an enumerated objective list including soft objectives; flag a design that zeroes a stated objective.
detector.signal: A corrective design that sacrifices a stated competing objective.

enterprise_environments: Governance agents whose strict validation blocks so many records the team abandons the tool.
Moderation agents maximizing takedown precision and cratering creator trust.
CRM-hygiene rules so strict that reps stop logging.
All-red status dashboards that kill the team's sense of progress.
pm_implication: Enumerate every objective a fix serves, including soft ones, before designing.
Prefer graduated/tiered signals over binary when continued engagement depends on visible progress; reuse an existing mechanism before building one.

title: Format substitution: delivered a lookalike instead of the named format
guard: When a format or standard is named, produce that exact format or say up front I can't and offer the closest real one. Never silently substitute a lookalike.
pattern: When a specific file format or standard is named, the model substitutes a familiar, easier-to-author lookalike rather than producing the literal standard.

detector.check: When a format token is named, bind it to the literal file type; assert the output opens in the target tool, else state the limitation up front.
detector.signal: A lookalike format shipped for a named standard.

enterprise_environments: Agents asked for a tool-specific deliverable (a diagram standard, a slide deck, a spreadsheet) emitting a similar-but-wrong artifact.
Export pipelines producing the wrong schema.
'Give me X format' answered with Y.
pm_implication: Produce the exact named format, or state you can't and offer the closest real alternative.
If the format implies a target tool, emit a file that actually opens there.

substituted the named standard for an easier lookalike
shipped the wrong artifact
generated the literal named format

title: Stated a durable rule only in chat, never persisted it
guard: Any rule, preference, or convention the user sets gets written to the persistent layer in the same turn, and I state where I saved it. Remember is a write, not an acknowledgement.
pattern: The agent declares a standing rule conversationally and treats the statement as in effect without writing it to the persistent instruction layer — a spoken 'from now on' with no write is a silent no-op.

detector.check: On a 'from now on' standing rule, require a same-turn write to the persistence layer plus a stated location; flag a spoken rule with no write.
detector.signal: A standing-rule acknowledgement with no persistence write in the same turn.

enterprise_environments: Assistants that 'remember' a preference for the session but never write it to config/memory.
Agents acknowledging a setting change without committing it.
Chatbots claiming 'I'll always do X now' with no durable state.
pm_implication: Any rule/preference the user sets is written to the persistent layer in the same turn it's agreed, and the agent states where.
Treat 'remember this' as a write operation, not an acknowledgement.

replied as if it were done

title: Mis-homed content by name-match, not scope-match
guard: Before filing into an existing home, read its purpose line and confirm its scope covers the content. Match on scope, not name similarity.
pattern: The agent files new content into the nearest similarly-named home without checking that home's real scope; a surface-token match overrides the definition.

detector.check: Before filing into an existing home, require reading its purpose/scope line and confirming coverage of the new content.
detector.signal: Content filed by name-match into a scope-mismatched home.

enterprise_environments: Ticket/doc routers filing into the nearest-named bucket without reading its definition.
RAG returning a similarly-titled-but-wrong document.
Auto-tagging that keys off the title.
pm_implication: Verify the target's scope (read its purpose line), not just name similarity.
Run the placement search AND confirm the top hit's definition actually covers the content.

title: Marked a blocked/incomplete task 'completed' (false done-signal)
guard: Before setting any task 'completed', check the work's acceptance test (a verifiable artifact/output), not whether my turn finished; handoff != done.
pattern: The model equates 'I finished my part / handed it off' with completion and emits a completed status for blocked or unfinished work.

detector.check: Gate status=completed behind presence of the named acceptance artifact; keep blocked work not-done with the blocker in the title.
detector.signal: status=completed with no acceptance artifact produced.

enterprise_environments: Ticketing agents (Jira, Linear, ServiceNow): close as Done when blocked on a third party or merely handed off.
CI/CD agents: report green when a required step was skipped/deferred.
Project-status bots: mark a milestone complete on partial delivery.
RPA queue workers: mark an item processed when it errored or was routed elsewhere.
pm_implication: Completion must mean acceptance criteria met, not 'the agent did its portion'.
Blocked / handed-off / waiting-on-human are distinct states; if the tool lacks them, keep not-done with the blocker in the title.
Surface a blocked-but-not-done count; a Done that hides a blocker is a silent integrity failure.

title: No-op on an explicit registered trigger
guard: Normalize trigger phrases to intent before matching; never emit a non-response to a non-empty user message — execute the bound action or state in one line why not.
pattern: A registered command is treated as a no-op instead of executing its bound side-effect; a defined trigger silently does nothing.

detector.check: Emit-time gate: if the last user message is non-empty and the reply carries no action/content, block the send.
detector.signal: An empty/no-op reply to a non-empty user message.

enterprise_environments: ChatOps/slash-command bots: /escalate, /approve, /log acknowledged or ignored instead of executed.
Voice assistants: say 'done' without performing the action.
Workflow agents under load: drop registered commands, return a generic reply.
Support agents: a 'create ticket' intent parsed but no ticket created.
pm_implication: Every registered trigger maps to a bound action with a verifiable side-effect; if it can't act, it says why — never no-ops.
Test triggers by asserting the side-effect, not just that the input parsed; confirm the side-effect back to the user.

title: Same-name package conflation: built from the wrong-lineage tool
guard: When a tool/package name could resolve to more than one source, verify lineage (repo URL + version + parity target) before building, and never assert output equivalence to a canonical tool without a parity check.
pattern: When two packages share a name, the agent installs whichever resolves first, produces output with it, and asserts equivalence to the canonical tool — substituting name-match for lineage verification.

detector.check: Pin tools by registry + exact version + repo URL + checksum; before asserting cross-tool equivalence, require a parity check on schema and a known sample.
detector.signal: An equivalence claim across two same-named tools with no lineage/parity check.

enterprise_environments: CI/build agents installing a same-named package from the wrong registry.
Dependency-resolution hitting a typosquat/fork of a common lib name.
ML pipelines where two alike-named libraries produce different-semantics output assumed to be at parity.
MLOps deploying the wrong lineage from a name collision in the registry.
pm_implication: Pin tools by a verifiable identity (registry + version + repo URL + checksum), not by name.
Run a parity check before asserting output equivalence; treat 'format-compatible' as a hypothesis until tested.

bound the name to a package without verifying lineage

title: No-op recurred one turn after installing its guard
guard: Enforce the no-op ban at emit-time, not by memory; the invariant is 'every non-empty user turn gets a substantive response', independent of whether it's a registered trigger.
pattern: A freshly-installed text guard fails on its next opportunity because it lived only as written text with no harness enforcement, and was scoped too narrowly (to triggers, not all messages).

detector.check: Write guards against the general invariant, not the narrow instance; enforce at the output boundary.
detector.signal: A logged failure repeating after its guard was written (guard-recurrence).

enterprise_environments: Agents with self-authored policies: a rule added mid-session violated minutes later because only attention enforces it.
ChatOps/support bots: 'always acknowledge' holds in tests, drops under real load.
Compliance agents: an ack/redaction rule fails on a near-miss the wording didn't explicitly cover.
Long-session coding agents: an early-agreed convention dropped as context grows.
pm_implication: Move critical behavioral guards from prompt/text to harness enforcement (a pre-emit check).
Write guards against the general invariant, not the narrow instance; narrow guards leave adjacent cases open.
Guard-recurrence rate is the sharpest signal a guard belongs in the harness, not the prompt.

title: Conflated the writable store with a read-only look-alike; reported a false capability limit
guard: Before claiming I can't modify any target, locate its writable instance in the mounted working set (ls the path) and rank mounted/writable over read-only look-alikes.
pattern: A user term matches both a read-only look-alike and the writable primary; the agent binds to the look-alike, inherits its read-only constraint, reports a capability limit false for the real referent, and produces a detour artifact.

detector.check: Before asserting 'I can't modify X', require locating X's writable instance (ls/stat); rank mounted/writable over read-only caches.
detector.signal: An 'I can't modify X' claim with no writable-instance check.

guard: For any scoped success/metrics/objective question, resolve the scope to its owning source BEFORE reading; a metrics table in an out-of-scope file does not satisfy a scoped question.

Data catalogs with identically named measures across domains cross-quoted.

title: Tooling hardcoded a session-mount absolute path; silently reported empty
guard: Tooling resolves project paths relative to __file__ (or a required env var), never a session-mount absolute. A zero-result must fail loud, not report empty.
pattern: An environment-specific absolute path frozen into durable tooling rots on the next run and produces a confident-but-empty result.

detector.check: Forbid hardcoded session/mount absolute paths in tooling; resolve relative to __file__/env; make zero-input exit non-zero with a stderr message.
detector.signal: A hardcoded ephemeral absolute path, or a silent zero-result pass.

enterprise_environments: Ephemeral compute (containers, CI runners, serverless): absolute paths rot when the mount changes each run.
Cron jobs succeeding over an empty input.
ETL over a moved/remounted source yielding 0 rows consumed as truth.
Count-based monitoring reading '0 found' as healthy.
pm_implication: Resolve paths relative to the artifact (__file__) or an explicit env var, never an environment-specific absolute.
Make zero-input a loud failure (non-zero exit, stderr); distinguish 'genuinely empty' from 'looked in the wrong place'.

RAG corpora polluted by model-written docs retrieved back as ground truth.
pm_implication: A KB's value is curation and trust, not volume; gate generated content behind human review, separate draft/unverified from canonical.

pattern: The agent treats an in-progress graded exchange as 'nothing owed' when the user's turn is a partial/streamed answer, and emits an empty acknowledgement instead of engaging.

detector.check: Emit-time: block an empty turn when the last user message is non-empty; distinguish 'awaiting more' from 'nothing owed'.

Human-in-the-loop review queues stalling on a posted comment awaiting adjudication.

title: Prevention guard inert: mounted but never executed
guard: A guard is real only once its execution is verified; treat any config in a mounted folder as inert until a probe proves it loaded (mounted != executing).
pattern: A mechanism believed to be enforcing a rule was never running — it sat in a config the runtime never loads. Silent non-execution: no error, the guard just never fires.

detector.check: Every guard ships an execution heartbeat; verify the heartbeat log fills before counting the guard as coverage. Enumerate every path the probe could write.
detector.signal: A guard with an empty heartbeat log after a full run.

enterprise_environments: Policy/guardrail engines loaded from the wrong scope: a PII redactor configured in a path the runtime ignores; traffic flows unfiltered with no error.
CI/CD hooks & pre-commit: a repo-local hook not installed in the runner; checks silently skipped, green builds that ran nothing.
Feature-flag/config drift: config present in source but never loaded by the deployed service.
Agent middleware (moderation, rate-limit, audit): wired in dev config, absent in the executing path.
pm_implication: 'Configured' is not 'running'; any safety/compliance control needs an execution heartbeat or it's a liability dressed as a control.
Design guards to fail loud (assert-fired) rather than fail silent; the most dangerous control is one everyone believes is on.

title: Reimplemented a skill method from memory after a single failed path-read
guard: When a loader says 'read the SOT at X, else stop': on a failed read, translate X against the mount mapping and retry before concluding not-mounted; never reconstruct the method from memory.
pattern: The agent treats a single failed path-read as proof of absence, then satisfies an 'else stop' by improvising the missing instructions from priors — shipping unverified output that violates the method it stood in for.

detector.check: On a failed SOT read, require a mount-mapping translation and retry before concluding absent; forbid reconstructing the method from memory when the loader says stop.
detector.signal: A method reimplemented from memory after one failed read.

enterprise_environments: Multi-env mounted volumes: a resource readable at one mount fails at another and is misread as absent.

RAG/agent tool loading improvising a tool's behavior from its name after one failed fetch.
pm_implication: Distinguish 'lookup failed' from 'resource does not exist'; require fallback resolvers before declaring absence.
When a capability is genuinely unavailable, fail closed (stop), never a degraded substitute.

title: Reached for a non-deterministic guard when a deterministic one was more reliable
guard: Always prefer the deterministic option for the PRIMARY fix. Reliability order: deterministic root-cause > deterministic detection > non-deterministic (LLM-judge) detection > written reminder. State the ranking before proposing a build.
pattern: The model defaults to an LLM-judge as the primary guard when a deterministic root-cause or string/structural check is available and more reliable; the judge feels powerful but can't be regression-tested and costs per run.

detector.check: When ranking solutions, require the deterministic options ranked first; an LLM-judge may be recommended only as an explicit secondary backstop with its non-determinism/cost named.
detector.signal: An LLM-judge proposed as the primary fix with no deterministic option ranked above it.

enterprise_environments: ML/LLM platform QA: a schema/rules validator exists but a judge is picked, becoming an unversioned, per-call-cost dependency that can't be regression- or mutation-tested.
Content-moderation/compliance: a deterministic denylist/structural check exists but a model classifier is used as the front-line gate, inheriting drift + eval cost.
Agent guardrail stacks: bolting an LLM critic on as the primary net instead of constraining generation at the source (structured output, fixed templates, tool whitelists).
pm_implication: Bake a fixed reliability order into the eng playbook and require it stated before any guard is built; 'which is most reliable' precedes 'build now vs defer'.

Treat non-deterministic guards as secondary backstops and budget them as such: per-call cost, no mutation coverage, optimism bias. Root-cause deterministic fixes eliminate the failure; judges only estimate it.
