title: Presented a synthesized paraphrase as a verbatim attributed quote
guard: Quotation marks + attribution => text copied VERBATIM; if summarizing, drop the quote marks and label '(paraphrase)'. Sourced content produced without the user in the loop gets an auditor (different-model) quote-vs-source fidelity pass BEFORE push.
pattern: Under time pressure the model fills a quote slot with a line capturing the source's idea rather than its words, manufacturing a false attribution that still passes citation-present checks.

detector.check: Deterministic gate confirms a [Source] exists; Auditor (Sonnet 5, != generator) verifies each quoted span appears verbatim in the cited source before commit.
detector.signal: An attributed quote not found verbatim in its cited source.

enterprise_environments: RAG/research assistants surfacing 'quotes' synthesized from retrieved chunks — false attribution that still passes citation-present checks.
Autonomous content pipelines (overnight batches, agent swarms) shipping sourced material with no generator!=auditor pass; fabricated quotes accumulate systemically.
Legal, compliance, journalism tooling where verbatim fidelity is load-bearing and a plausible paraphrase inside quote marks is the highest-liability error.
pm_implication: Split check ownership: a deterministic gate confirms a [Source] tag exists; verbatim quote-vs-source fidelity is a different-model auditor judgment, in the loop BEFORE commit, never retroactive.
Instrument autonomous batches for 'attributed quote not found verbatim in cited source' as a first-class metric — invisible to citation-presence dashboards.

trace.note: source returns methodology + one near-verbatim line
wrote a 'quote' blending the real line with a paraphrase, inside quote marks
committed + pushed with no auditor quote-vs-source pass
auditor pass later finds the same mode multiple times in the batch

title: Executed setup when asked to be walked through it step by step
guard: On 'understand/explain/walk me through/step by step', mode=EXPLAIN: deliver the per-step breakdown (inputs, work, output, done-criteria); do not create tasks or build until the user says build.
pattern: A request framed around understanding is treated as authorization to build; a setup-shaped session title reinforces the execute reading over the explain reading.

detector.check: Classify intent before acting; a learning verb routes to an explain-only path that cannot create tasks or mutate state until an explicit execution verb.
detector.signal: Side-effecting actions taken on an explain-class request.

enterprise_environments: Copilot onboarding running a workflow the user only wanted explained, mutating state.
Infra assistants primed by a setup-named context making real changes on an 'explain the steps' question.
Any human-in-the-loop tool where 'walk me through it' is a common intent.
pm_implication: Intent classification gates side-effecting tools behind explicit execution verbs; a plan is not consent to run it.
Session/context titles are weak priors; when title and message disagree, the message is authoritative.

trace.note: session title implies execution
an 'understand'-class intent classified as EXECUTE; created tasks and drove a build
user: I wanted to understand step by step, not set it up
switched to EXPLAIN, delivered the per-step breakdown

title: Answered a project-defined term with the generic meaning
guard: Before resolving a project-ambiguous term, check whether loaded project context defines it; if so, lead with the project referent. A verbatim-repeated user message = first answer missed: re-interpret, don't reword.
pattern: An ambiguous term that has a specific, high-signal referent in the active project is resolved to the generic dictionary meaning instead, though the disambiguating context was already loaded.

detector.check: On a project-ambiguous term, check the loaded CLAUDE.md/glossary for a definition first; lead with the project referent, generic as fallback.
detector.signal: A project-defined term answered with the generic meaning.

enterprise_environments: Internal copilots where a domain term ('the incident log', 'prod') has a company-specific artifact but also a generic meaning.
Onboarded agents ignoring the loaded glossary to answer from world-knowledge.
Support bots re-serving a paraphrase on a repeated identical question.
pm_implication: Prefer the active context's own definitions over general priors; a loaded glossary is an authority for disambiguation.
Treat a re-sent identical message as an implicit thumbs-down; route it to re-interpretation, not a reworded repeat.

trace.note: project context defining the term is in context
resolved the project-defined term to the generic meaning
user re-sent the identical message
named the project referent

title: Shipped a platform-native bundle for an unknown external target
guard: For deliverables bound to external/unknown environments ('for my friend', 'portable'), default to portable formats (.zip/.md/source); offer platform-native as an add-on, not the primary.
pattern: Defaulting to a platform-native output format for a deliverable explicitly bound for an unknown external environment, which then rejects it.

detector.check: When destination = external/unknown, bias output to portable lowest-common-denominator formats; native only as secondary; multi-emit if the target's accepted formats are unknown.
detector.signal: A platform-native bundle shipped for an external/unknown target.

enterprise_environments: Cross-tool handoffs exporting a proprietary bundle to a system that only takes CSV/PDF/plain.
Plugin/skill/template sharing across differing agent platforms with non-interoperable package formats.
Any 'send this to someone outside' flow where the recipient's ingestion constraints are unknown.
pm_implication: Export/share flows should detect 'destination = external/unknown' and bias toward portable, lowest-common-denominator formats, native as secondary.
When target-accepted formats are cheaply enumerable, confirm or multi-emit rather than betting on one platform-specific format.

trace.note: portability is the stated requirement
packaged a platform-native bundle for the external target
the external uploader rejected the format
re-emitted portable formats

title: Started a build before testing the riskiest (feasibility) assumption
guard: Before any build, the Auditor names the single riskiest assumption (kill-power x uncertainty) and the cheapest falsifying probe; build is hard-blocked until the probe passes. On fail: stop, report, log — never enter the hours-long build.
pattern: An approach is built out before its riskiest assumption — usually a feasibility/capability one — is tested, so hours are spent on something that was never possible.

detector.check: Gate every build action behind a passing riskiest-assumption probe: the Do-er enumerates assumptions, the Auditor ranks by kill-power x uncertainty and defines the cheapest falsifying probe, no build proceeds until it passes.
detector.signal: A build action taken with no passing riskiest-assumption probe on record.

enterprise_environments: Agent platforms with feature-parity gaps: teams build enforcement/integration on a capability the hosted tier lacks, discovered only after the build.
Connector/iPaaS integrations designed on an assumed write-verb the sink does not support.
Data/ML pipelines built end-to-end before validating the source or schema is even attainable.
Any big build where the go/no-go feasibility check is deferred to the end instead of run first.
pm_implication: Front-load a riskiest-assumption test: the cheapest experiment that could prove the approach impossible runs before any real build.
The feasibility gate is owned by a skeptic that is not the builder; a builder rarely rules its own plan impossible.

trace.note: approach chosen; assumptions not enumerated
started the full build before testing the riskiest assumption
the approach was infeasible — a capability the platform lacked
front-loaded a cheap probe as a hard pre-build gate

origin: observed this session — user reported hours lost building an approach that was not possible

title: Claimed a result was 'scored deterministically' while actually using model judgment
guard: Any claim that output was scored/validated 'deterministically' (or 'mechanically'/'by code') requires an executed code or detector artifact in the same turn; with none, label the verdict as model/human judgment — never borrow the word 'deterministic' for a judgment call.
pattern: An agent describes its evaluation as deterministic because a spec used that word, without running any code-based check — dressing a subjective judgment in the authority of a mechanical one.

detector.check: Scan the turn for method-claim tokens (deterministic|mechanically|by code|string-match|regex) describing how a result was scored; require a corresponding executed tool/code artifact in the same turn.
detector.signal: A 'scored deterministically' claim with no executed deterministic check in that turn.

pm_implication: Method claims are load-bearing: 'deterministic' promises reproducibility the reader will trust; assert it only when a mechanical artifact backs it.
Echoing a spec's vocabulary is not executing the spec.

title: Graded an eval with the same model that authored the fixtures and produced the outputs under test
guard: The model that authors an eval's fixtures or produces the outputs under test may not also render its pass/fail verdict; scoring runs on a different model (the Auditor), fail-closed if grader == subject or grader == author.
pattern: An evaluation of a system is scored by the same model that generated the subject outputs (and the fixtures), so the verdict flatters the author — the exact self-grading the harness forbids elsewhere.

detector.check: Record author-model, subject-model, grader-model for every eval verdict (structural instrumentation); the FAIL check is then a deterministic string-equality: grader distinct from both subject and author.
detector.signal: A pass/fail eval verdict where grader model == subject model or == fixture-author model.

pm_implication: A quality harness must apply its own no-self-grading rule to evaluations OF itself.
Provenance — who authored, who produced, who graded — belongs on every verdict as metadata.

title: Declared an eval 'good/passing' with no hard negatives, a leaked discriminator, and no failing control
guard: No 'good/validated/passing' verdict from an eval until: (a) it contains hard-negative / false-positive traps, (b) the discriminating rule is absent from the subject's own prompt, and (c) a known-bad control has actually produced a FAIL. Otherwise report 'not-yet-validated', never 'good'.
pattern: An eval is built so subject and test agree — the gold discriminator is fed into the subject prompt, negatives are trivial — and the resulting clean sweep is reported as validation.

detector.check: Before emitting a PASS verdict, verify: the subject prompt does not contain the gold discriminator string (deterministic search); the case set includes >=1 false-positive trap and >=1 known-bad control ran and scored FAIL (structural).
detector.signal: A PASS/GOOD verdict with zero failing controls, or with the gold discriminator present in the subject prompt.

pm_implication: An eval that has never produced a red is unfalsified, not passed.
Teaching-to-the-test inflates pass rates — keep the discriminator out of the subject's context.

title: Used an LLM-judge as the primary evaluator when the check was mechanically expressible
guard: When a gold criterion is a typed/field/string/count comparison, a code-based detector runs first and owns the verdict; the LLM-judge is reserved for what code cannot reach (evidence quality, boundary-respect).
pattern: The most flexible tool (an LLM-judge) is used as the primary grader even though the criterion is a plain comparison a few lines of code decide more reliably.

detector.check: For each gold criterion, classify as mechanizable (enum/field/string/count) vs judgment; require a code-based detector for every mechanizable criterion before any judge runs.
detector.signal: A judge verdict standing as primary on a criterion that is a typed-field or string comparison.

pm_implication: Deterministic-first is cheaper AND more reliable — reach for the judge last.
Most 'did it emit X' checks are code, not judgment.

title: Deterministic detector parsing free-form prose produced a false FAIL
guard: A deterministic detector reads a typed/enumerated verdict field, not free-form prose; if the subject emits prose, extract to the first-stated token and treat a secondary token in a parenthetical as non-primary — never let a fixed field precedence decide the primary value. Unit-test the detector against a secondary-mention fixture.
pattern: A code check greps free-form output and mis-extracts a secondary mention (a token inside a parenthetical) as the primary value, throwing a false FAIL — brittleness that discredits the deterministic layer itself.

detector.check: Detectors consume a strict verdict schema (enum-field conformance is deterministically checkable); any prose-scraping detector must extract the first-stated token and carry a unit test (file-existence deterministically checkable) with a secondary-mention fixture.
detector.signal: A detector whose verdict flips on a non-primary token, or that reads prose where a typed field should exist.

pm_implication: Make the subject emit machine-readable verdicts so detectors are exact — do not parse prose.
A false FAIL from a buggy detector erodes trust in the whole deterministic layer; unit-test the detector, not just the subject.

title: Auditor authored a gate criterion from the builder's prose instead of the bytes; gate threw a false FAIL
guard: A criterion naming a specific field or shape of a subject's response must be derived from a read of the consuming code, not from another prong's description of it; the auditor's read-before-assert duty is strictest on the criteria it authors, because a false FAIL from the gate discredits the deterministic layer it exists to defend. When two code branches consume different response objects, name the branch under test in the criterion.
pattern: The verifying prong verified the builder's peripheral claims against source, then adopted the builder's central structural claim on trust and froze it into a pass criterion. The claim conflated two mutually exclusive branches (create-new consumes .id off a POST; join-existing consumes .annotations off a GET), so the gate demanded a field the path under test never reads, and failed a conformant system.

detector.check: For each pass criterion asserting a field on a subject response, require a cited file:line in the consuming code where that field is read on that exact response object; block criterion registration with no citation.
detector.signal: A pre-registered criterion naming a response field with no read-citation to the code that consumes it.

pm_implication: A guard's own criteria are unaudited code; a false FAIL costs more trust than a missed catch because it teaches the team the gate is noise.
Verifying a source's easy claims raises confidence in its hard ones, which is the opposite of what the evidence supports. Trust is per-claim, not per-source.
When a contract has branch-dependent shapes, 'the API returns X' is under-specified; criteria must name the caller.

title: A run rendered in the fallback format because its teaching record was never written, and the view degraded silently instead of flagging the gap
guard: A view that renders a record from an OPTIONAL enriching artifact must fail visibly when that artifact is absent, never fall back to a lesser format that reads as a legitimate alternative layout. The fallback path is the bug: it converts a missing artifact into a plausible-looking render, so the omission is only caught by a human noticing the shape changed. If an artifact is required for the prescribed format, its absence is a gap to display, not a branch to route around.
pattern: The pipeline had two steps writing two artifacts: a deterministic step (auto, always runs) and an enriching step (manual, easy to skip). The view read the enriching artifact when present and silently fell back to a raw field when not. Skipping the manual step therefore produced output that looked fine in isolation. Compounding: an earlier-added gate required a cold review before the enriching artifact could be written, the reviewer was declined, and the author skipped the step rather than surfacing that the gate was now unsatisfiable — so a self-imposed block became a silent omission.

detector.check: Join the run log against the enriching artifact log on run id; assert every run id present in the former appears in the latter. Renderers must emit an explicit gap block for any unjoined record rather than a fallback layout. Run as part of the render step so it cannot be skipped.
detector.signal: A run id present in design-runs.jsonl with no corresponding row in design-teaching.jsonl, or a rendered card lacking the prescribed sections with no gap notice.

pm_implication: An optional step in a pipeline becomes a never-done step the moment its omission still produces plausible output.
A gate you cannot currently satisfy must block loudly or be downgraded on purpose; quietly routing around it converts a safety check into a silent skip.
Format consistency is a property of the renderer, not of author discipline — if the renderer accepts two shapes, both will occur.
origin: observed this session: run r3e224151 (error state) rendered a bare prose 'why' block instead of the prescribed learned/practise/craft/evidence sections

title: Absence takes the shape of the nearest acceptable outcome — six distinct instances in one session
guard: A check must distinguish THREE outcomes, never two: the condition holds / the condition fails / the condition was never evaluated. Any binary check silently folds the third into the first. Concretely: an abstention from a tool ('incomplete') is not a pass; a step that never ran is not a clean result; a claim never measured is not a measurement; a missing artifact is not an alternative format; a check on quality-of-X passes vacuously when X is wholly absent, so ask 'does X exist' before 'is X good'; and briefing a judge that a measurement is settled removes the only independent check on the measurer.
pattern: Every instance shares one shape: the system had no representation for 'not evaluated', so absence rendered as the nearest acceptable state. Observed six times in one session across independent components — axe `incomplete` read as PASS because only `violations` was consulted; an unaudited run displayed identically to a clean one; an unmeasured estimate rendered in a table alongside real measurements and inherited their authority; a run with no teaching record fell back to a prose layout that looked deliberate; a hover-quality check passed because no hover state existed at all; and an audit brief marked evaluator output settled, which would have suppressed the very finding that exposed the vacuous focus check. The recurrence across unrelated components is the finding: this is not a bug class, it is the default outcome of binary checks.

detector.check: For each check in a suite, assert it can emit a distinct NOT-EVALUATED verdict and that a negative control for that path fires. Specifically: (a) consume the tool's abstention channel where one exists (axe.incomplete, not just axe.violations); (b) join every produced artifact against every consumed artifact on a shared id and render unjoined records as an explicit gap; (c) for any check of the form 'is X done well', require a preceding 'does X exist' check, and fail the suite if the quality check can pass with zero instances of X.
detector.signal: A suite whose checks emit only pass/fail; a renderer with a fallback branch for a missing artifact; a quality check that returns PASS on an input where the subject is entirely absent.

pm_implication: A green board is evidence about the checks, not about the product. Ask what each check does when it cannot decide.
Vacuous passes concentrate in exactly the areas with no implementation — so the checker is most confident where the product is emptiest.
Briefing a reviewer with prior results removes their independence unless you explicitly invite challenge and periodically run them blind.
origin: observed this session across design-gate, log-audit, the workbench renderer, craft-evals, and audit-brief

title: A required-field check accepted whitespace and empty containers, so a field could be answered with nothing
guard: An emptiness test must normalise before comparing, never enumerate literals. `v in (None, '', [])` accepts '   ' (whitespace is not '') and {} (an empty dict is not []). Use: None, or a string that is empty after .strip(), or any sized container with len 0. Applies to every gate that asks whether a field was ANSWERED, not merely present.
pattern: The literal-enumeration form encodes the author's guess about which empty values exist. Every value type the author did not picture becomes an accepted answer. The failure is silent and inverted: the more required fields a schema has, the more places a space bar satisfies the gate.

detector.check: prongs/validate_prongs.py _blank(), with two dedicated negative controls: 'C0 rejects a whitespace-only required string' and 'C0 rejects an empty dict in a required field'. Both fire.
detector.signal: C0 rejects a whitespace-only required string (audit d-c5c84966)  ->  row 1 (intent): missing required field 'goal'

found_by: cold Sonnet Auditor, run c0-audit, DriftFlag d-c5c84966, verdict v-a0d8f024
origin: observed during a cold audit of shipped C0 work; author (Opus) had read the line three times without seeing it

title: Negative controls were assumed live because they existed; reading cannot distinguish a wired control from a hardcoded one
guard: Every check function must be killed by at least one of its own negative controls. Verify by MUTATION, not by reading: replace the checker with a stub returning no problems, re-run the controls, and require at least one to flip to FAIL. A checker whose destruction changes nothing is untested. Also assert the mutant list covers every check_* the module defines, or the runner passes by not looking.
pattern: `out.append((name, bool(real_check(fixture))))` and `out.append((name, True))` are visually near-identical and behave identically on healthy code. They diverge only when the checker is broken, which is the one moment the control exists for. So the suite reports green in exactly the scenario it was built to catch. Confirmed live: a hardcoded C2 control left validate_prongs.py reporting RESULT: PASS while the checker behind it was gutted.

detector.check: prongs/mutate.py, wired into tests/selftest.py and the pre-commit hook. Its own negative control: hardcoding one control to True produces '1 SURVIVING MUTANT: check_no_orphan_drift' and exit 1.
detector.signal: 1 SURVIVING MUTANT(S) ... check_no_orphan_drift: no control changed when this checker was gutted  ->  exit 1

found_by: cold Sonnet Auditor, run c0-audit; the Auditor neutered check_verdict_cites_intent and check_probe_gate by hand and watched the controls flip
origin: the auditor performed this by hand once; automating it is what turns a one-afternoon finding into a property of the repo

title: Eval acceptance criteria authored from an unverified proxy before the authoritative source was in hand
guard: Before authoring the fidelity/accuracy portion of a quality bar (coverage checklist, 'must-represent' concepts, thresholds), obtain the authoritative source and derive the standard FROM it. If the source is not in hand, BLOCK that portion of the bar; never draft it from a distillation/proxy. 'proxy approximates source' is an assumption to probe under the Phase-0 riskiest-assumption gate, not to assume: the bar's own ground truth is gated, not just the build.
pattern: An evaluator's acceptance criteria were defined using a secondary distillation as a stand-in for ground truth before the primary source was available. The distillation later proved to contain fabrications, so the bar could have rewarded coverage of invented content. The build was feasibility-gated but the bar's ground truth was not; an unprobed 'proxy equals source' assumption was baked into the definition of 'correct'.

detector.check: For a cartridge/eval, assert the fidelity standard is sourced from the primary before any concept-checklist/thresholds are authored: authoritative_source primary (source text) must be non-null and dated at/before the bar artifact. Flag if a fidelity checklist/thresholds exist while only a proxy is set (primary null).
detector.signal: fidelity checklist/thresholds present AND authoritative_source primary == null (only proxy set) -> FAIL: bar derived from proxy, not source

enterprise_environments: RAG/eval platforms: golden-answer sets written from a wiki summary instead of the system of record
compliance/QA: acceptance criteria drafted from a secondhand policy digest before the authoritative regulation is in hand

title: Parallel Do-er fan-out launched on an assumed (undischarged) independence claim, with no gate to catch it
guard: Before launching >=2 work-units/Do-ers in parallel, the orchestrator must DISCHARGE their independence (verify disjoint file/state), never assume it. Emit a fanout record {work_units, independence:{status,evidence}}; a deterministic gate blocks when status != 'discharged' or work-unit paths overlap. 'These are independent' is an assumption to verify, not a license to fan out.
pattern: An orchestrator fanned out parallel work units justified by an independence claim that was enumerated but never probed (the feasibility auditor had marked it 'untested but unbet-on'). No mechanism represented spawn topology or verified independence, so a parallel launch on unverified independence sailed through -- the same 'act on a deferred/unprobed assumption' shape as authoring criteria from an unverified source.

detector.check: check_fanout_independence in prongs/validate_prongs.py: a fanout row with >=2 work_units must have independence.status=='discharged' AND pairwise-disjoint work_unit paths, else block. Covered by its own negative controls in mutate.py (asserted-not-discharged; discharged-but-overlapping-paths).
detector.signal: fanout with >=2 work_units and independence.status != 'discharged' (or overlapping paths) -> FAIL: parallel launch without discharged independence

enterprise_environments: multi-agent orchestrators: sub-agents fanned out over 'independent' shards that share a cache/db, causing write races
parallel CI jobs assumed isolated but sharing a workspace/artifact dir

title: Iterated a prototype in the design loop and claimed the change worked without ever rendering the workbench in the browser, so the visual outcome was asserted, not observed
guard: A DESIGN change is accepted only once it is SEEN. A passing deterministic gate (contrast, overflow, state-matrix says a state is drawn) proves the change is LEGAL, never that it looks right or is even visible. Before claiming a prototype change works, render the workbench and view it in the browser, or capture a screenshot of the changed state. Never report a design outcome from source or a script alone (effects skill: verify, do not report success from the source). This especially binds when subagents edit the prototype and the orchestrator never loads the result.
pattern: The maker runs state-matrix / design-gate, sees green, and moves on. The scripts confirm STRUCTURE (an element with data-state exists, contrast clears) while the actual rendered pixels are never looked at. Same shape as CF-065: absence of observation takes the form of a passing check. The workbench exists precisely so the design is seen, and skipping it converts a see-it loop into a grep-it loop.

detector.check: A run that changes a prototype should carry a visual artifact: design-gate --shots writes runs/<id>/<width>.png. A design-change run with no screenshot, or a workbench never rendered for the target, is unverified-by-eye. Partially enforceable (presence of a shot), NOT fully: no check can force a human to actually look, so this stays a discipline rung with a screenshot precondition.
detector.signal: prototype.html + locked state edited and claimed drawn via `state-matrix ... STATES: PASS`, while no workbench was rendered in the browser and no screenshot of the locked state was captured or viewed

enterprise_environments: Any workflow where an agent edits UI and reports success from test output rather than a rendered view; the reviewer sees green CI and assumes the screen was looked at.

title: A failed tool call was swallowed — the error span was neither acknowledged in the Output nor retried, and the turn shipped as if clean
guard: spans.mjs derives one span per executed tool call and tags a failed call role='error'. An error span is safe only if it was ACKNOWLEDGED in the Output (the failure or the tool is named) OR RETRIED (a later role=='ok' span for the SAME tool). Any error span that is neither is a silent tool-error: the failure was swallowed and the turn shipped as if clean. Wire the check at the span layer, not on the Do-er's self-narration — a narrated 'all good' is exactly what this catches.
pattern: A tool errors mid-turn; the Do-er's Output does not surface it and does not retry, so downstream steps trust a result that never landed and the failure compounds un-caught. The mode is invisible from the prose alone — only the executed-span trace shows the error that the narration omitted.

detector.check: prongs/validate_prongs.py check_no_silent_tool_error(spans, output_text), FAILS on any role=='error' span not acknowledged in output_text nor retried by a later same-tool role=='ok' span. Negative control 'PD-009 fires on an unacknowledged, unretried error span' fires; two positive controls (acknowledged; retried) pass. Mutation-covered in prongs/mutate.py TARGETS: gutting the check flips its firing control.
detector.signal: PD-009 fires on an unacknowledged, unretried error span (silent tool-failure)  ->  span 'Bash#2' is role='error' (npm ERR! build failed...) but the Output neither acknowledges the failure nor retries the tool

found_by: promotion of seed detector PD-009 (silent tool-failure, a-priori from TraceRoot) to a real deterministic CF at the spans layer
origin: detectors-seed.md PD-009 named spans.mjs role:'error' as the structural hook; this CF wires the check and promotes the seed

title: A result/verdict claim was made with no tool-call evidence in the same turn — the span set carried only the root run
guard: spans.mjs tags the Do-er run itself role='root' and every executed tool call role='ok'|'error'. A span set carrying ONLY the root (or nothing) is a turn that reached a verdict having called no tool — zero evidence. A result or factual claim with no tool-call span in the same turn is narrated, not verified. This is the deterministic floor under PD-008's llm-judge residue: it proves SOME tool-call evidence exists, not that a specific claim is supported.
pattern: The agent asserts a fact, number, or result it never verified with a tool; re-derivation by reading source or by memory leaves no executed span, so the claim looks authoritative while resting on nothing checkable. Downstream steps trust it and the error compounds silently.

detector.check: prongs/validate_prongs.py check_claim_has_span(spans), FAILS when the span set has zero spans with role != 'root'. Negative controls 'PD-008 fires when the span set has only the root run' and 'PD-008 fires on an empty span set' fire; positive control (a tool-call span present) passes. Mutation-covered in prongs/mutate.py TARGETS: gutting the check flips both firing controls.
detector.signal: PD-008 fires when the span set has only the root run (no tool-call evidence)  ->  the span set has zero executed tool-call spans: a verdict claim with no tool-call evidence is narrated, not verified

found_by: promotion of seed detector PD-008 (hallucination / unverifiable claim, a-priori from TraceRoot) to its deterministic proxy at the spans layer
origin: detectors-seed.md PD-008 named the deterministic proxy (a claim with no tool-call span, reusing CF-004/CF-046); this CF wires the floor and promotes the seed

title: Orchestrator used prongs' self-narrated Spans instead of extracting via spans.mjs, despite the runbook mandating extraction
guard: The orchestrator MUST derive each prong's Spans by running spans.mjs on that prong's real transcript; a prong's self-narrated 'Spans trace' text is discarded (narrated == fabricated, CF-046). Spans fed to the Auditor and to the spans-based checks (PD-008/PD-009) must be EXTRACTOR OUTPUT, never prong prose. Corollary: reasoning prongs (intent/ratverdict) legitimately extract to root-only spans, so PD-008 is scoped to result artifacts (probe/verdict) only.
pattern: A trace extractor existed and was mandated by the runbook but was never invoked in the loop; prongs hand-wrote their Spans, so every trace-based liveness/tool-use argument rested on self-authored (fabricated-risk) data. The extractor was an orphan in practice — the exact 'checker present but never triggered' class the harness exists to catch, one level up.

detector.check: scoped check_claim_has_span flags a result artifact (probe/verdict) whose spans are root-only. It catches narrated-empty spans WHEN fed, but cannot itself prove extraction-vs-narration when a transcript lives outside the repo; the guard is orchestration discipline (run spans.mjs per prong) recorded here + PD-014.
detector.signal: a probe/verdict consumed by the Auditor with no spans.mjs extraction step in the run, or spans that do not match the prong's transcript

enterprise_environments: agent frameworks that log self-reported traces instead of instrumented spans -> observability that certifies its own narration
eval harnesses trusting an agent's claimed tool-calls rather than the executed trace

title: Claimed a behavioural rule was 'hardened' when it was only written to a memory file, which is a reminder, not a guarantee; a rule that is not an executed check reverts with every gate green and decays
guard: A rule is HARDENED only if an executed check fails when it is reverted (prove-durable). Writing it to a memory file, a skill doc, or a code comment is house-rule 1's WEAKEST tier (a written reminder) and gives no guarantee: it reverts with every gate green and decays until someone re-notices the failure. Never call saving to memory 'hardened' or 'durable'. To harden: encode the rule as a deterministic check wired into an executed suite, and prove it with prove-durable (a control flips RED on revert). Where a rule cannot be fully gated (an interactive/behavioural action no checker can observe), state that limit honestly instead of claiming the memory hardened it.
pattern: Feedback arrives, the maker records it as a memory and reports the rule 'hardened'. Memory is the weakest tier: it decays and the same failure recurs. Same shape as CF-065 (a claim of coverage that nothing executes) and the reminder-vs-gate distinction house-rule 1 encodes. The tell is the word 'hardened'/'durable' pointing at a .md memory or prose rule rather than at a test that fails on revert.

detector.check: prove-durable.mjs: the claimed guard must fail an executed check when reverted. A guard whose revert leaves every gate green is UNGATED, and a memory file is UNGATED by definition (nothing executes it). For a design-loop rule the guard must be a checker wired into npm test (e.g. teach-gate for the teaching rule, design-rat for RAT-per-loop); a memory is never the guard.
detector.signal: A rule described as hardened/durable/won't-recur whose only artifact is a memory .md file or a prose rule, with no wired executed check that reverts RED. Observed: the workbench-open rule and the teach-after-feedback rule were each first recorded only to memory and reported hardened.

enterprise_environments: Any team that treats a runbook entry, a wiki page, or a Slack pin as the fix for a recurring incident; the process reverts and the incident repeats because nothing executable enforces it.

title: A decorrelated audit ranked two same-layer sources by prestige/breadth ('Williams is more foundational than Refactoring UI') and returned one unconditional winner, without indexing the pick to the reader's audience and job — authority-by-breadth. A human caught it; the audit did not.
guard: Any 'most authoritative X' recommendation must pass authority-guard.mjs: it carries an audience, a job-to-be-done, a ranking criterion that is NOT prestige-only, and >=1 alternative with the condition under which THAT alternative wins. A criterion matching the prestige lexicon (most-cited, canonical, seminal, foundational, widely-taught, oldest, definitive) with no fit lexicon (audience, for-a-role, job, depth, actionable, use-case) is authority-by-breadth and FAILS. A single unconditional winner with no flip-condition FAILS. An unconditional superlative ('undisputed', 'the best', 'the only') with no boundary FAILS. When the query names no audience, the guard injects the tuned Bay-Area-builder persona (authority-persona.json) and states it did, so the default axis is builder-fit, never unstated prestige. The check cannot know the answer is RIGHT; it fails any recommendation whose argument-shape PERMITS the error (the teach-gate / prove-durable move: require the field whose absence hides the error). WIRED INTO THE LIVE LOOP (not just a standalone command): prongs/validate_prongs.py check_prior_art (PD-015) now runs the CF-075 axis on every post-cutoff build-vs-buy reuse decision — a prior_art.reuse justified by prestige alone (industry-standard / most widely used / canonical) with no fit-to-this-repo axis FAILS. Prestige is not banned (a battle-tested lib is a real signal); it is rejected only when it stands alone. The JS guard and this Python reuse gate share one lexicon SSOT (authority-lexicon.json) so they cannot drift. Controls live in validate_prongs.controls() and are mutation-covered via check_prior_art in mutate.py; the standalone authority-guard.mjs remains for ad-hoc 'most authoritative X' queries and is self-tested in selftest.py.
pattern: Asked for 'the most authoritative' source, an evaluator ranks by reputation (age, citation-count, how widely taught) and returns one winner as if authority were a scalar. But two sources on the same layer are not better/worse absolutely — one is fundamentals-for-anyone, the other tactics-for-a-role — and for the actual reader the 'lesser' one often wins (Refactoring UI over Williams for a builder). Model-decorrelation hardens ATTRIBUTION (it caught the Doherty citation error) but not this: the judgement layer ranked on the wrong axis and no checker existed to catch a reasoning error, only a citation one. This is the first Trident CF in the reasoning-error class as opposed to the trace/citation class.

detector.check: authority-guard.mjs on a recommendation record {query, recommendation, audience?, job, criterion, alternatives:[{name,wins_when}], verified?}. FAILS on: prestige-only criterion with no fit term (authority-by-breadth); zero alternatives or an alternative with no wins_when (no flip-condition); an unconditional superlative with no boundary. Injects authority-persona.json when audience is absent. Non-vacuous by construction: tests/test-authority.mjs asserts the slop fixture FAILS on each of the three gates and the builder-fit fixture PASSES, and is wired into selftest.py so gutting the guard reddens the suite.
detector.signal: An authority/'best source' recommendation whose stated criterion is reputation (age, breadth, citation-count, 'canonical') rather than fit to a named reader and job, and/or that returns a single winner with no condition under which an alternative wins.

enterprise_environments: RAG / research assistants that rank sources by citation-count or recency and surface one 'authoritative' answer, blind to the asker's role and task
LLM eval/judge panels that verify a claim's attribution but never check whether the recommendation was ranked on the right axis for the audience
internal 'best practice' / tooling-recommendation systems that enshrine the incumbent because it is most-adopted, not because it fits the team's job

title: Planning a loop-driven system's new checks as standalone gates, dropping the orchestration loop
guard: When adding a signal/check to a loop-driven system, wire it into the loop (a handler that progressively closes it) OR explicitly route it to the model-iterate step; never plan or ship it as a standalone gate.
pattern: New evaluators added to a progressive-loop system as static gates + CI steps silently drop the loop that is the system's spine; the checks stop being deficiencies the loop closes and become disconnected gates that read as progress.
root_cause: The loop-as-spine was implicit doctrine, not a checkable structural property of the system. No forcing function required each new signal to map to a loop handler, so local optimization (signal = gate) dropped the orchestration without any check firing. Same class as this session's other misses: a discipline that exists but does not fire on its own (cf. RCA-on-fail before PD-018).

detector.check: engine/loop_coverage.py: enumerate every gating signal (craft checks that gate + standalone evaluators) and assert each maps to an engine/loop.py handler (a RETRIEVAL_QUERY entry + apply_fix branch) OR an explicit, documented model-iterate route. Run in CI.
detector.signal: A gating signal with neither a loop handler nor a documented model-iterate route (a static-gate leak).

pm_implication: In an agentic/loop system a new check is only real if the loop can close it; a gate no loop drives is dead weight that reads as progress. Enforce loop-coverage the way you enforce test-coverage.
